title: Goldeneye: Source
notes:
Switch to Desktop.

Download the 7zip file from here: 
https://www.moddb.com/mods/goldeneyesource/downloads/gesv506linuxwindowserver7z

Unzip the file to get a "gesource" folder.

Go to /home/your_name/.steam/steam/steamapps/ and check 
if a folder called "sourcemods" is in the directory
If not then create the folder.

Cut or Copy & Paste the gesource folder directly into the sourcemods folder.

Close down Steam and relaunch Steam to refresh the list, 
Goldeneye: Source (v.5.0) will appear when searched in the library.

Install Source SDK Base 2007 

Once both are installed, change the compatibility layers of both 
Goldeneye: Source and SDK Base 2007 to: Proton Experimental (or Proton Hotfix)

Switch Back to Gamemode.

Launch Goldeneye: Source.

Note: Finding servers  on the internet will result with an error message 
"SteamID logged in a different location" and aborts connecting to server, unknown cause as of now.

Host and Guest:

Host: Create Server, Enable 'Run on LAN', —, Create Server.
Guest: Find Server, Uncheck 'Show Map List'. Refresh, Highlight the host lobby, Connect.

title: F.E.A.R
notes:
CAUTION: You will be using a keygen to generate a code allowing multiplayer to work, Steam and GOG versions of F.E.A.R do NOT offer a CD key installed with the game, using the same CD key will result with guests or hosts unable to join the same lobby,
this is the only way to get it set up and running currently.

Both Host & Guest need to do this set up, once the CD key is initialized you won't have to do this set up again unless you uninstall the game, the directory containing the CD key file gets removed when uninstalling.

-------------------------------------------------------

Switch to Desktop Mode.

Download Keygen:
https://tinyurl.com/yutrf664

Extract the .rar file.

Open Steam.

Add non-steam game.

Click Browse
Add the "rld-fearkgr.exe"

Add selected programs.

Search "rld-fearkgr.exe" in the Steam Library
Right click and select Properties.

Change compatibility to: Proton 9.0.4.

Launch rld-fearkgr.exe

Once the keygen launches hit Generate,
ensure that the keygen did not generate the same code per device (very rare to happen),
copy the code it generated.

Paste the code into a Steam Note
(You can't copy and paste the code in the game - you will have to enter it manually.
Having it here will be good to reference back to later)

Switch back to Gaming Mode.

Once in the gamingmodeui.

Launch F.E.A.R.

Go to Multiplayer.
Hit Yes to switch to Multiplayer.
Host Settings.
CD Key.
Input the CD Key found in the Steam Overlay (SHIFT+TAB) or STEAM button, Notes.
Click OK once done.
Exit the game.

You are now free to go offline and ready to play.

Host & Guest:
Switch to Multiplayer, hit YES.
(In offline LAN mode, loading Multiplayer will take a little longer)

Host:
Click Host.
Where it says LAN Only, switch it to YES.
—,
Launch

Guest:
Switch Source from Internet to LAN (Under Find Servers).
Find Servers.
Highlight Host Server.
Join.

title: Alien Swarm: Reactive Drop
notes:
Host & Guest: Add "-console" to launch options.
Host: type: "sv_lan 1" then "map ", select any map on the list, Submit.
Guest: Same "connect HOST_IP", Submit.
(Note: You can change the level and/or game type by calling a vote in the menu)

title: Call of Duty: World at War
notes:
CAUTION: This method will render the Steam Deck as the dedicated host device. 
We do NOT recommend running the game and the dedicated server on the Steam Deck simultaneously. 
Instead it is best to use separate devices to connect to the dedicated server.

The following guide is to get your dedicated server up and running, 
assuming you already have experience setting up security measures such as: 
whitelists, passwords, and changing IPs or ports. 
For additional security, consult any supplementary guides 
to ensure your server remains safe from unknown intruders.

-------------------------------------------------------

Co-op Campaign and Zombies mode on the standard launcher does not work
You will be greeted with an error message of 
"authentication failure" and the guests will be booted after you start the lobby match.

-------------------------------------------------------

Please ensure using the internet beforehand for installing and configuring the dedicated server


Dedicated Server:
Download Call of Duty World at War in the Steam Library.

Choose: 'Launch Call of Duty World at War Multiplayer' when prompted

Allow the required anti-cheat (Punkbuster) and DirectX installs

Create a Custom profile when prompted

When prompted to create an online profile, choose no.

Once at the Title Screen go to "Start New Server"

Where it says "Dedicated" switch the option to LAN
Adjust other settings to suit your preference

Start

The game will switch into a console menu, this is the dedicated server.

Guests:
Switch to Proton Experiemental compatibility.
Launch Call of Duty World at War.
Choose: 'Launch Call of Duty World at War Multiplayer' when prompted
Allow the required anti-cheat (Punkbuster) and DirectX installs

You can now go offline and won't have to do this set up again.

Note: If you get an error message regarding: Can't login to online servers.
Click away from the message and you'll have access to the main menu.

Create a Custom profile when prompted.
When prompted to create an online profile, choose no.
When in the Title Menu, select "Find Game".
At the top menu where it says "Source", switch it to: "LAN".
Hit 'Quick Refresh' or 'Refresh List' in the top right.
The dedicated server will now show up.
Highlight the server then click "Join Server".

Guest: find games, select "Lan" tab, highlight lobby, Connect.

title: Unturned
notes:
CAUTION: This method will render the Steam Deck as the dedicated host device.
We do NOT recommend running the game and the dedicated server on the Steam Deck simultaneously.
Instead it is best to use separate devices to connect to the dedicated server.

The following guide is to get your dedicated server up and running,
assuming you already have experience setting up security measures such as:
whitelists, passwords, and changing IPs or ports.
For additional security, consult any supplementary guides
to ensure your server remains safe from unknown intruders.

-------------------------------------------------------

Please ensure using the internet beforehand for installing and configuring the dedicated server

Dedicated Server:
Download the Unturned Dedicated Server in the Steam Library.

Set The Unturned Dedicated Server proton compatibility layer to: 'Proton Experimental'.

In the Unturned Dedicated Server launch options, add the line: 
'"-OfflineOnly" and "-NetTransport=SystemSockets" without the quotation marks

Launch the Dedicated Server,
You can choose to launch the DC in GameMode or Desktop Mode

Leave it a little bit until the console has stopped writing text.

Guests:
Launch Unturned.
Choose: 'Play without BattleEye Anti-Cheat' when prompted.
Play.
Multiplayer.
Click 'All LAN' Button in the second bottom bar.
If it doesn't show up, go back and choose.
'Connect Directly' then input the Host IP.
Click the server to instantly Join.

title: STAR WARS™ Battlefront (Classic, 2004)
notes:
CRITICAL INFO:
It is essential you do this set up beforehand as internet is required for downloading the following plugins:
1.2 Version of Star Wars Battlefront I (2004)

Installing this mod will prevent any access to online matchmaking / lobby joining.
Any previous data after installing this modification will be wiped
To undo the mod proceed with the following:
Uninstall Star Wars Battlefront I (2004) From the Library
Switch to Desktop Mode
Open the Steam Library Folder, Locate Star Wars Battlefront I (2004), 
right click it and select the browse local storage.
Delete the remaining files in the folder.
Reinstall Star Wars Battlefront I (2004).

Both host and guests install the mod.

-------------------------------------------------------------

Switch to Desktop Mode.

Download the .rar file from this link:
https://archive.org/details/star.-wars.-battlefront.v-1.2.-gog

Extract the .rar file into a folder, open up that folder and inside you should see an .exe file

Go to Steam, click on "Add a Game" and "Add a non-steam game"

Navigate to the folder where that .exe file is and select it, click on 
"add selected programs" the .exe file should now show up in your steam library.

Search it in the Search Bar, Right Click, Hit Properties.
Select Compatibility and Select "Proton Experimental"

Launch the software and install as normal
Note: Make note of the directory install folder

Once all is installed, locate the 1.2 Star Wars Battlefront Build as mentioned above.

Copy every single file in the directory (GameData, Install, Manuals folder and all of the files under it)

Go to your Star Wars Battlefront I (2004) Game Directory.
To find the Star Wars Battlefront I (2004), go to your Steam Library. 
Right click Lethal Company, hover over manage and click "Browse local files"
A window will pop up showing the Star Wars Battlefront I (2004).

Paste all of the files you have copied from the 1.2 Star Wars - Battlefront folder into the directory.
Make sure to write into & overwrite every file and folders

Your game is now on the 1.2 build, you won't have to do this step again if you wish to remain offline for LAN.

Note: You can copy the 1.2 build onto a external SSD/USB to share to guests 
so they can overwrite their current build, ensure guests back up their files in a separate folder beforehand!

--------------------------------------------

Host & Guests:

Host:
- Create New Profile / Choose Existing One (Not Flingle)
- Multiplayer
- Direct Connection
- Create
- Create your custom settings (--)
- Launch


Guest:
- Create New Profile / Choose Existing One (Not Flingle)
- Multiplayer
- Direct Connection
- Join
- Enter Host IP

Note: The game matches will run at 20fps. 
I won't be supplying a mod that bypasses the FPS lock. 
But there are other guides out there!

title: Valheim
notes:
CAUTION: This method will render the Steam Deck as the dedicated host device.
We do NOT recommend running the game and the dedicated server on the Steam Deck simultaneously.
Instead it is best to use separate devices to connect to the dedicated server.

The following guide is to get your dedicated server up and running,
assuming you already have experience setting up security measures such as:
whitelists, passwords, and changing IPs or ports.
For additional security, consult any supplementary guides
to ensure your server remains safe from unknown intruders.

-------------------------------------------------------

Dedicated Sever:
Download Valheim Dedicated Server From Steam Library

Switch to Desktop Mode

Click Steam, Find Valheim Dedicated Server in Steam LIbrary, 
right Click and Click 'Browse Local Files'

Right Click 'start_server.sh' and open it in a text editor (Kate by default)

Replace the line 'Public [0]' to 'Public [1]'

Remove '-crossplay' as well
Optional: replace 'secret' with any password you would like

Save & Exit

Return back to GameMode

Go offline, Start up 'Muon' and allow other devices to connect

Boot up Valheim Dedicated Server, wait until the script has finished.
You will get prompts saying server can't connect 
but ignore those as it's trying to reach the steam backend servers

Guests:
Start.
Create Character or Select Character.
Cancel on New World.
Switch to Join Game Tab.
Click 'add server',
Input HOST_IP (Port number will be defaulted)
Highlight the server and click 'connect'
When joining put in the password
Default will be 'secret' if host hasn't changed it
You will then be joined into the server!

title: Halo: The Master Chief Collection
notes:
WARNING:
It is essential you do this set up beforehand as internet is required for the following:
Xbox Live Account Login.

CRITICAL NOTE: You will need to sign in to Xbox Live again if your account is 
left inactive for too long - usually by a month, make sure to check before going offline.

Launch Halo The Master Chief Collection enabling Easy Anti-Cheat.

Make sure your Xbox Live account is signed up and logged in.
It is IMPORTANT you do this step as finding players on the local network will require Xbox gamer tag searching.
Make sure your Xbox live account is friends with every user beforehand 
as not adding the guests to your friends list on Xbox Live may cause complications.

---- You are now free to go offline once this step is complete ----

----------------------------------------------------------------

Host & Guests:

Launch Halo The Master Chief Collection Anti-Cheat Disabled (Mods and Limited Services)

Note: logging into the main menu will take a little while longer, but be patient.

Once in the main menu:
Options & Career --> Settings --> Networking
Switch Network from Xbox Live to LAN.
Press B to exit out and Save.

Use the touch screen to click your Xbox Live Gamertag in the top right 
or click the top right to show your Gamertag.

See if any of your friends show up on the LAN network.
Make sure guests join who ever is Hosting Muon.

Choose between multiplayer, custom multiplayer or campaigns.

title: Abiotic Factor
notes:
CAUTION: This method will render the Steam Deck as the dedicated host device. 
We do NOT recommend running the game and the dedicated server on the Steam Deck simultaneously. 
Instead it is best to use separate devices to connect to the dedicated server.

The following guide is to get your dedicated server up and running, 
assuming you already have experience setting up security measures such as: 
whitelists, passwords, and changing IPs or ports. 
For additional security, consult any supplementary guides 
to ensure your server remains safe from unknown intruders.

-------------------------------------------------------

Dedicated Server:
Download the Abiotic Factor Dedicated Server from the Steam Library.

Set The Abiotic Factor Dedicated Server proton compatibility layer to: 'Proton Experimental'.

Launch the Dedicated Server,
You can choose to launch the Dedicated Server in GameMode or Desktop Mode.
It takes a little bit to start up, until the console has stopped writing text.
In GamingMode it will not show the text and be in a dark screen, wait a minute then try connecting.

Guests:
Launch Abiotic Factor.

Press No to allow Offline Mode.

Enter the Facility.
Join a Server.
Direct Connect.
Switch to 'IP Address' tab.
Enter the Muon HOST_IP into the IP Bar.
Port will be defaulted to 7777 on server.
Join.

title: Synergy
notes:
Host: Create Server, Choose "LAN" under Type, Start Lobby
Guest: Find Servers, Select "Lan" tab, Refresh, Join HOST Server

title: Wreckfest 2
notes: Despite direct local IP connection options, users must be online to use any multiplayer functionality.

title: Killing Floor
notes:
Host: Create a lobby, Check "LAN" and select "Listen" to start lobby.
Guest: Find Multiplayer, Choose "Lan" tab, Join HOST Lobby.

title: Resident Evil 5
notes:
WARNING: This tutorial will be using modifications that will bypass the Steam Networking API using SmartSteamEmu.
We will not be held responsible for any banned accounts due to this method.

CRITICAL NOTE: Installing this mod will prevent any access to online matchmaking / lobby joining.
Steam Achievements will not be earned through out the game.
Any previous data after installing this modification will be wiped, personal data can be restored if you used Cloud Saves as a default
To undo the mod proceed with the following:
Uninstall RE5 From the Library
Switch to Desktop Mode
Open the Steam Library Folder, Locate RE5, right click it and select the browse local storage.
Delete the remaining files in the folder.
Reinstall RE5.

This installation will require using the internet for mod installation. Do this before going offline

--------------------------------------------------------------

Mod Installation:

Note: We recommend using a mouse & keyboard as you'll need to do some text editing with a keyboard and copy & pasting with a mouse.

Both Host & Guest will have to install this mod

Switch to Desktop Mode and ensure RE5 is installed on both devices:

Go to this link to download the LANFix for RE5:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SOV860y1Xzb0lsga4bvtyHXEogYMetfg/view?usp=sharing

Once downloaded, extract the containment files into a 're5' folder.

Inside there you should see a 'settings folder', click it open.
Inside you should see two files called: 'user_steam_id.txt' and 'account_name.txt'

With 'user_steam_id.txt' edit it in Notepad/Kate, change only 2 numbers within that line of text - BUT MAKE SURE THE CODE ISN'T THE SAME AS YOUR GUEST/HOST.
Then Save & Exit

Next is 'account_name.txt', open that and in it you'll get a default name, change it to any name you like - AS LONG AS IT ISN'T THE SAME AS YOUR HOST/GUEST.
Then Save & Exit.

Copy all the files in the LANFix folder into your RE5 directory, make sure to override every file.
You shall see RE5LAN.exe, Launcher.exe files and a folder called SmartSteamEmu now in your RE5 directory folder.

You are all set and are free to go back to Gaming Mode and will not have to do this set up process again if you want this game to remain in offline LAN mode.
Game saves will all be local on the device.

---------------------------------------------------------------

Ad-Hoc Connection for Host & Guest:

Host:
New Game/Continue, Under Networking, Switch Network Selection to: Local, Start Game, Wait in the Item Selection Menu.

Guest:
Join Game, Select Quick Match, the Host lobby will be found, join session.
Host will have to accept the Guest joining.

title: Lethal Company
notes:
CRITICAL INFO:
It is essential you do this set up beforehand as internet is required for downloading the following plugins:
BepinExPack, LCDirectLAN.

It is recommended that both host and guests install the mods in case host preferences change.

In case you prefer to work visually. Here are some alternative guides to help you get set up.

Install Bepinex_win_x64 file for Lethal Company (Visual Guide):
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3121888924#:~:text=2-,Modding%20LC%20Manually,by%20clicking%20on%20Manual%20Download

Install LCDirectLAN:
https://github.com/TIRTAGT/LCDirectLAN/wiki/Installation

-------------------------------------------------------------

Set Up:

Switch to Desktop Mode.
First off you will need to download BepinEx.
Use this link and MAKE SURE you download the: BepinEx_win_x64 version, Linux one's won't function
https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/

Next up, to get BepinEx installed with Lethal Company:
To find the Lethal Company folder, go to your Steam Library.
Right click Lethal Company, hover over manage and click "Browse local files"
A window will pop up showing the Lethal Company directory.
Extract all of the files from the Bepinex_win_x64 into the Lethal Company folder.
Right click the Lethal Company game in Steam Libarays again and now click "Properties".

In the bar called Launch Options.
You'll need to put in this code into that bar:

WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp.dll=n,b" %command%

Note: This will allow the BepinEx executable to launch properly

Launch the game ONCE.
Once it's launched, close the game down.

If BepinEx is installed correctly, check steamapps/common/lethal company/BepInEx

New folders such as:
config
plugins
patchers
cache
and LogOutput.log

Will now show up.

Next you'll need to Download LCDirectLAN.
https://github.com/TIRTAGT/LCDirectLAN/releases

Once Downloaded, extract the files into a folder and name the folder LCDirectLAN
Lastly, copy and paste that folder you just made into: steamapps/common/lethal company/BepInEx/plugins.

If all is correct, launch the game.
Select "LAN"
A new option in the menu called: "Direct LAN Join" will now appear.

You're now ready to play Lethal Company with Muon!

--------------------------------------------

Ad-Hoc for Host & Guests:

Host:
Start Game.
Select "LAN"
Host.
Select "Allow Remote Connections"
Start Game.

Guest:
Start Game
Choose LAN
Click Direct LAN Join
Enter Host IP (Found on Host Muon Plugin)
Default Port: 7777
Click Join.

Note: If you are experiencing blurry or low res text.
Go to Properties in the Steam Game Libaray
Click on 'Game Resolution' and set it to: 1280x720.

Text doesn't function well in Lethal Company with 1280x800.

title: Call of Duty® 4: Modern Warfare® (2007)
notes:
CAUTION: This method will render the Steam Deck as the dedicated host device.
We do NOT recommend running the game and the dedicated server on the Steam Deck simultaneously.
Instead it is best to use separate devices to connect to the dedicated server.

The following guide is to get your dedicated server up and running,
assuming you already have experience setting up security measures such as:
whitelists, passwords, and changing IPs or ports.
For additional security, consult any supplementary guides
to ensure your server remains safe from unknown intruders.

-------------------------------------------------------

Please ensure using the internet beforehand for installing and configuring the dedicated server

Dedicated Server:
Download Call of Duty 4 in the Steam Library.

Choose: 'Launch Call of Duty 4 Modern Warfare Multiplayer' when prompted

Allow the required anti-cheat (Punkbuster) and DirectX installs

Once at the Title Screen go to "Start New Server"

Where it says "Dedicated" switch the option to LAN
Adjust other settings to suit your preference

Start

The game will switch into a console menu, this is the dedicated server.

Guests:
Switch to Proton Experimental.
Launch Call of Duty 4.
Choose: 'Launch Call of Duty 4 Modern Warfare Multiplayer' when prompted.
Allow the required anti-cheat (Punkbuster) and DirectX installs.

You can now go offline and won't have to do this set up again.

When in the Title Menu, select "Join Game".
At the top menu where it says "Source", switch it to: "Local".
Hit 'Quick Refresh' or 'Refresh List' in the top right.
The dedicated server will now show up.
Highlight the server then click "Join Server".

title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell Chaos Theory®
notes: Host & Guest: Co-Op: Choose "Co-Op" and hit "LAN" after, Verses: Create a profile then select Play on LAN
